metaTitle: Boiler Reset Button Not Working | What It Usually Means
metaDescription: Boiler reset button not working? Learn what that usually means, what is safe to check and when to call an engineer.
h1: Why Is My Boiler Reset Button Not Working?
intro: If the boiler reset button is not clearing the fault, or the boiler resets and immediately locks out again, the problem is usually deeper than a simple control glitch.
safetyWarning: Repeated resets can mask the real fault and may leave the boiler locking out again straight away.
symptoms: The boiler resets and then locks out again
You still have no heating or hot water after resetting
safeChecks: Check whether the boiler has power and the controls are calling for heat
Check the display for a fault code before pressing reset again
Check the pressure gauge for obvious low pressure
doNotDiy: Do not keep pressing reset repeatedly
Do not open the casing to chase the internal fault
Do not ignore a boiler that is locking out again immediately
typicalFixes: Repairing the underlying ignition or control fault

sections.heading: What the reset button is actually doing
sections.body: The reset function tells the boiler to try its start-up sequence again after a lockout. If the original fault is still present, resetting will not solve it.

sections.heading: Why the reset may appear not to work
sections.body: The boiler may be immediately detecting the same fault again, which is why the reset feels ineffective or only works for a few seconds.

sections.heading: When to stop resetting and call an engineer
sections.body: If the reset does not clear the lockout, or the boiler trips again right away, book a repair visit so the actual cause can be diagnosed safely.
